zh.Swinden, Craven, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾斯文登
zh.Gisburn, Yorkshire, England, United King: 英國英格蘭越可爾吉斯本
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Banester於1464年出生在英國越可爾斯文登時，她的父親Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon28歲，母親Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend28歲。她於從1484年到1486年與Sir William Lister在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1508年1月1日在英國越可爾米德普享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Banester于1464年出生在英国越可爾斯文登时，她的父亲Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon28岁，母亲Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend28岁。她于从1484年到1486年与Sir William Lister在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1508年1月1日在英国越可爾米德普享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Banester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bannister  .
History: The naturalist John Banister (1650–92) was born in Gloucestershire, England, and came to VA in 1678.

variants.name: Bannister
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French banaste, banastre ‘covering for a cart or wagon; basket’, i.e. a large wicker container. In the 12th century a Norman family of this name had estates in Orne, Normandy, and in England. Ricardus Banastre appears in charters relating to the Earls of Chester c. 1120–29. With what sense the Norman surname was acquired is unknown. It can hardly have been occupational, contrary to Reaney's view that it denoted a basket maker. It is possible that many or even all of the later bearers of the surname were descended from this knightly family. However, several men with this surname in the 14th-century Poll Tax Returns are described as servants or agricultural laborers, while Ricardus Banastr', recorded in 1381 was a butcher. It is conceivable that these men took their name from Middle English banastre, a borrowing of the French word, and that it referred to a basket or hamper they used in their work. Alternatively, they may have belonged to branches of the knightly family that had fallen in the social scale. The term denoting a stair rail is unconnected with this name; it was not used before the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Banester was born in 1464, in Swinden, Yorkshire, England, her father, Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon, was 28 and her mother, Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, was 28. She married Sir William Lister from 1484 to 1486, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 1 January 1508, in Midhope, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Banester est née en 1464, à Swinden, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Lister du 1484 au 1486, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1508, à Midhope, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Banester 1464 in Swinden, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon 28 und ihre Mutter Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend 28. Sie hat von 1484 bis 1486 in Yorkshire, England Sir William Lister geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Januar 1508 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Midhope, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Banester è nata il nel 1464 nel Swinden, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Lister da 1484 a 1486, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 gennaio 1508, nel Midhope, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Banesterが1464年にイギリスヨークシュアイアスウィンデンで生まれた時，父Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindonは28歳，母Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seendは28歳であった。から1484年まで1486年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir William Listerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1508年1月1日にイギリスヨークシュアイアミドhオペで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Banester(이)가 1464년 영국 요르크실르 스윈든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1484년 부터 1486년 까지 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir William Lister(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1508년 1월 1일 영국 요르크실르 미드홉에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Banester nasceu em 1464, em Swinden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Lister de 1484 a 1486, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de janeiro de 1508, em Midhope, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Banester родилась в 1464 г. в местности свинден, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Lister с 1484 по 1486 в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 января 1508 г. в местности мидхоп, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Banester nació en 1464, en Swinden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord Thurstan de Bannistre of Swindon, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Lady Alice Mauleverer of Stoakes Hall, of Seend, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sir William Lister desde 1484 hasta 1486, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de enero de 1508, en Midhope, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
